18   P, Q, R and S show the graphs of

                           y ൌ (cos x) cos x, y ൌ (sin x) sin x, y ൌ (cos x) sin x and y ൌ (sin x) cos x

     for 0 < x < ʌ in some order.
                 2

      P                                                             Q
           1                                                               1


          0.5                                                           0.5



            0                 π             π             π                0                  π            π          π
                              6             3             2                                   6            3          2


      R                                                             S
           1                                                               1


          0.5                                                           0.5



            0                 π             π             π                0                  π            π          π
                              6             3             2                                   6            3          2


     Which row in the following table correctly identifies the graphs?

                y ൌ (cos x) cos x            y ൌ (sin x) sin x            y ൌ (cos x) sin x            y ൌ (sin x) cos x
     A                 P                            Q                            R                             S

     B                 P                            Q                            S                             R

     C                 Q                            P                            R                             S

     D                 Q                            P                            S                             R

     E                 R                            S                            P                             Q

     F                 R                            S                            Q                             P

     G                 S                            R                            P                             Q

     H                 S                            R                            Q                             P




                                                              20

stem.rawText:
P, Q, R and S show the graphs of

                           y ൌ (cos x) cos x, y ൌ (sin x) sin x, y ൌ (cos x) sin x and y ൌ (sin x) cos x

     for 0 < x < ʌ in some order.
                 2

      P                                                             Q
           1                                                               1


          0.5                                                           0.5



            0                 π             π             π                0                  π            π          π
                              6             3             2                                   6            3          2


      R                                                             S
           1                                                               1


          0.5                                                           0.5



            0                 π             π             π                0                  π            π          π
                              6             3             2                                   6            3          2


     Which row in the following table correctly identifies the graphs?

                y ൌ (cos x) cos x            y ൌ (sin x) sin x            y ൌ (cos x) sin x            y ൌ (sin x) cos x

solution.rawText:
Let us substitute values into the four equations to begin with.
When x = 0, we have

                                       (cos x)cos x = 11 = 1
                                        (sin x)sin x = 00 = ?
                                        (cos x)sin x = 10 = 1
                                        (sin x)cos x = 01 = 0

This shows that graph Q is y = (sin x)cos x . (We do not know what (sin x)sin x is when x = 0,
but from the graphs given, since three of the functions have value 1 at x = 0 and only one is 0,
it must be 1.)
Next, let us consider the value at x = π2 :

                                       (cos x)cos x = 00 = ?
                                        (sin x)sin x = 11 = 1
                                        (cos x)sin x = 01 = 0
                                        (sin x)cos x = 10 = 1

Again, as only one graph has value 0 at x = π2 , graph P depicts (cos x)sin x . (We did not actually
need to calculate the final line of this list, as we have already identified this function as being
graph Q.)
So we are left with (cos x)cos x and (sin x)sin x as graphs R and S in some order. Let us put in
the value x = π6 , as that is clearly different between the two functions. We have
                                                            √
                                                       √ ! 23
                                              cos x      3
                                      (cos x)       =
                                                        2
                                                       1
                                              sin x    1 2
                                       (sin x)      =
                                                       2

The first of these looks horrible, but we can approximate the value of the second of these; it is
just √12 ≈ 0.7, so this must be graph S. Similarly, (cos x)cos x ≈ 0.7 at x = π3 , which is graph R.
Therefore the graphs are:

                                       (cos x)cos x : graph R
                                       (sin x)sin x : graph S
                                       (cos x)sin x : graph P
                                       (sin x)cos x : graph Q

which is option E.
